sessions.intent: Design Setup.goal run-once execution system for Runtime2

before.assumptions: Runtime1 had RunSetup/HasExecuted system. Runtime2 needs equivalent with OBP-compliant design.

sessions.plan: Trace runtime1 setup system, design OBP-compliant Setup object with Executions smart collection, write handoff spec for coder.

actions.context: Architect handoff spec for Setup.goal system

after.health: Design spec ready for coder implementation
after.notes: Key decisions: Setup is an object not a filter, Executions wraps sqlite table, context.Setup propagates through goal.call, Steps.RunAsync owns iteration.

sessions.intent: Implement Setup.goal run-once execution system per architect handoff

before.assumptions: Architect spec is sound. EngineGoals.Setup is currently IEnumerable<Goal>, needs to become Setup object. Steps needs RunAsync. PLangContext needs Setup property.
before.risk: Low — well-defined spec, familiar patterns (SqliteDataSource). Medium risk on excluding setup goals from regular lookup (may break callers).

sessions.plan: Create Setup/this.cs (uses engine.System.DataSource, no separate Executions class), add PLangContext.Setup, modify EngineGoals.Setup property, add Steps.RunAsync with setup check, refactor Goal.RunAsync to delegate, wire up in Executor.Run2, write C# tests.

actions.context: Setup object with Goals, RunAsync, IsExecuted, Record — uses engine.System.DataSource instead of separate Executions class
EngineGoals.Setup becomes object, PLangContext.Setup added, Steps.RunAsync with run-once check, Goal.RunAsync delegates to Steps (OBP rule 5), Executor.Run2 calls Setup before main goal
9 tests: goal ordering, exclusion from lookup, IsExecuted/Record, skip already-executed, hash change re-run, context propagation, Clone
Updated existing Setup test to use goals.Setup.Goals.ToList() instead of goals.Setup.ToList()

after.notes: Simplified from architect's design: no Executions class, uses engine.System.DataSource directly. Discussed with Ingi — table name is 'setup', using existing DataSource was always the intent.
